ACWA Power, Masdar, TotalEnergies, EDF,: ACWA Power, Masdar, TotalEnergies, EDF, Voltalia

intro.heading: One of the most dynamic economies in Eurasia
intro.p1: Since 2017, Uzbekistan has carried out one of the largest economic transformations in Eurasia. Liberalisation of the currency market, tax and judicial reforms, the opening of capital markets and privatisation have turned a once-closed economy into one of the most dynamic investment destinations in the region. Today it is Central Asia’s largest market by population — around 37.7 million people — with steadily growing domestic demand.
intro.p2: 2025 Confirmed this trajectory. GDP growth reached 7.7% — above most forecasts; inflation fell to 7.3% and unemployment to 4.8%. Fitch raised the sovereign rating to BB for the first time in seven years, S&P affirmed BB, and Moody’s kept Ba3 while upgrading the outlook to ‘positive’. Accession to the World Trade Organization is being completed — the largest structural opening of the economy since the currency liberalisation of 2017.

timeline.title: Trajectory of reforms
timeline.subtitle: From opening the economy to a capital market

y2017.text: Liberalisation of the currency market, the start of systemic reforms; GDP around $56 bn.

y2019.text: Establishment of the Foreign Investors Council under the President — an institutional platform for dialogue with investors.

y2024.text: Energy reforms; record investments by international companies in the green economy.

y2025.text: Fitch and S&P ratings raised to BB; foreign investment inflow ~$42 bn; GDP growth 7.7%.

y2026.text: Completion of WTO accession; creation of the Tashkent International Financial Centre; target — GDP above $150 bn. Over nine years, around $125 bn in foreign investment was attracted, against ~$4.5 bn in 2017.

intro.para1: The Foreign Investors' Council under the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan is an institutional platform for direct dialogue between the Government of Uzbekistan and investors operating in the country. Established by Presidential Resolution No. PP-4519 dated November 13, 2019.
intro.para2: The Council's activities are aimed at identifying key barriers to attracting investments, developing solutions jointly with government agencies to eliminate them, and ensuring a favorable investment climate. The Chair of the Council is the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan; the Co-Chair is the President of the EBRD.

legalBasis.heading: Legal Basis and Institutional Status
paragraphs: The Foreign Investors' Council under the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan was established by Presidential Resolution No. PP-4519 dated November 13, 2019 as a consultative and advisory body for direct dialogue between foreign investors, international financial institutions and the Government of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

As the Council's activities expanded, the need arose for a permanently functioning operational body. By Presidential Resolution No. PP-226 dated July 18, 2025, the proposal of the Council members and international financial institutions to establish the Secretariat of the Council in the form of a non-governmental non-profit organization was supported.

In execution of this resolution, on November 4, 2025, the Association of Legal Entities (Association, Union) "Foreign Investors Council", TIN 312 551 219, was registered — a legal entity performing the functions of the Secretariat of the Council.

intro.text: The Council's experts are recognized international and local specialists who provide expert support to the working groups and initiatives of the Foreign Investors Council. The Council highly values their contribution to the development of Uzbekistan's investment policy and recognizes their professional competence.

local.title: Local experts

members.intro: The members of the Foreign Investors Council are leading international companies, banks and financial institutions making strategic investments in the economy of Uzbekistan. Membership reflects a long-term commitment to the country’s development and provides a direct channel of dialogue with the Government on investment policy matters. The Council brings together 41 members from 19 countries, representing 23 sectors of the economy.

fullMembers: Investments or projects amounting to ≥ 50 million USD

intro.p1: The Secretariat of the Foreign Investors Council is a permanently functioning operational body that ensures the continuity and consistency of the Council's work between sessions. The Secretariat organizes plenary and interim meetings, coordinates the activities of the sixteen working groups, maintains a daily dialogue with Council members and foreign investors, collects and analyzes initiatives to improve the investment climate, and ensures their advancement into the relevant state policy.
intro.p2: With the acquisition of legal entity status, the Secretariat gained the institutional autonomy necessary to effectively fulfil its mandate and to engage with international financial institutions.

aziz.description: Doctor of Economic Sciences with over 20 years of experience in corporate governance, investment policy, and strategic consulting. He has extensive experience working with international investors, development banks, and government institutions. He leads the development of investor relations, policy coordination, and institutional development.

avazbek.description: Avazbek Mullajonov is a specialist with more than 15 years of experience in the field of foreign economic relations, investments, and energy. He worked as Deputy Head of the Investment Climate Department and Head of the Cooperation Department with the Republic of Turkey at the Ministry of Investments and Foreign Trade of Uzbekistan. He holds a master's degree in International Business from Ajou University and a degree in Management from Tashkent State University of Economics.

hakimniyaz.description: Hakimniyaz Kaipbergenov graduated with honors from MGIMO in Moscow with bachelor's and master's degrees in international law. He has experience working at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Uzbekistan as well as at an international law firm.

vladislav.description: Graduated from the Tashkent branch of the University of Westminster in "Business Management and Finance" and speaks three languages fluently. He also has practical skills in effectively working with clients, conducting negotiations, building trust-based relationships, and efficiently solving problems.

jurabek.description: Jurabek Babaev is a specialist with experience in marketing, PR, and international business. He currently heads PR and communications at the Foreign Investors Council under the President of Uzbekistan. Throughout his career, he worked in business development, international trade, and the textile industry, and also worked as a lecturer at the Tashkent State University of Economics.

madina.description: Madinakhon holds a Bachelor's degree in Business and Economics and a Master's degree in Agribusiness and Investment Activities. She has worked as a manager at the Association of Private Tourism Organizations of Uzbekistan, where she coordinated events aimed at the development of national and international tourism.

strategicBanner.text: Creation of the Regional Alliance of Investment Councils of Central Asia and the Caucasus (RAIC-CAC) to form a competitive investment environment and cross-border coordination.

items.i0: Establishment of the Regional Alliance of Investment Councils of Central Asia and the Caucasus and holding annual Council forums
items.i1: Creation of a legislatively established Project Office for coordinating the implementation of priority investment initiatives
items.i2: Creation of a public digital platform for comprehensive investment activity assessment «FIC Investment Dashboard»

items.i0: Admission of the private sector to electricity distribution activities (amendments to legislation)
items.i1: Exemption of projects with foreign financing from domestic reinsurance requirements
items.i2: Development and publication of the national Master Plan for energy sector development (generation, transmission, distribution)
items.i3: Establishment and regular revision of license fee rates for electricity generation
items.i4: Reform of the electricity generation licensing procedure: phased document submission and timeline coordination

items.i0: Introduction of risk-based flexibility in remote access rules for banks
items.i1: Building capacity of domestic financial institutions and developing capital market infrastructure
items.i2: Expanding blended finance instruments and deepening cooperation with international financial institutions
items.i3: Aligning banking reforms with infrastructure financing needs in the energy sector
items.i4: Introduction of a legal framework for the flexible purchase and sale of credit portfolios
items.i6: Granting the right to use previously acquired foreign currency and prepaid import advances
items.i7: Reducing the tax burden on foreign capital: withholding tax and capital gains tax
items.i8: Introduction of instruments to incentivize local investor participation in the capital market (tax benefits for individuals and institutions)
items.i9: Introduction of a legal framework ensuring flexible purchase and sale of credit portfolios
items.i10: Introduction of a regulatory framework for mortgage loans secured by real estate
items.i11: Creation of a regulatory framework ensuring the sale of non-performing loan portfolios (NPL)

items.i0: Development of tax provisions for public-private partnership (PPP) agreements
items.i1: Clarification of land tax and property tax exemptions for energy storage system projects
items.i2: Elimination of the requirement to register a permanent establishment in each region of presence (amendment to Art. 36 of the Tax Code)
items.i3: Improvement of the mechanism for making payments for land plots
items.i4: Introduction of a VAT exemption mechanism for the import of equipment not produced in Uzbekistan
items.i5: Introduction of separate VAT thresholds for offline and online trade (individual entrepreneurs and self-employed)
items.i6: Revision of the procedure for granting land tax benefits during the construction period
items.i7: Revision of the mechanism for applying a coefficient of 0.3 to land tax for rainfed and pasture zones
items.i8: Application of land tax exemption for RES projects to the entire area of the land allocation
items.i9: Introduction of a tax deduction for expenditures on corporate social responsibility (CSR) projects

items.i0: Simplification of visa procedures for foreign investors when replacing a passport
items.i1: Reduction of customs duties on raw materials for tobacco production
items.i2: Extension of the implementation period for the updated design of excise stamps on tobacco products
items.i3: Introduction of a legal stability guarantee mechanism for foreign investments
items.i4: Legislative consolidation of the principle of legislative stability for investment and PPP projects
items.i5: Regulation of accounting for share premium contributions
items.i6: Establishment of mandatory deadlines for adopting secondary legislation to investment laws
items.i7: Improvement of the mechanism for supporting the implementation of PPP projects
items.i8: Optimization of the procedure for reviewing and signing PPP projects
items.i9: Formation of an Interdepartmental Expert Committee for analyzing environmental standards in urban planning
items.i10: Consider the possibility of implementing international standards for green infrastructure and buildings
items.i11: Development and adoption of the Presidential Decree «On transitional provisions for the application of PD-46»

items.i0: Preparation of a new edition of the Corporate Governance Code
items.i1: Establishing a minimum gender standard on the boards of directors of public companies
items.i2: Transformation of corporate governance in State Enterprises and banks through the introduction of RBC
items.i3: Ensuring convenience of participation in the general meeting of shareholders and exercise of related shareholder rights
items.i4: Creation of fully functional supervisory boards
items.i5: Ensuring the obligation of management bodies and controlling persons of a joint-stock company to act in good faith and reasonably in the interests of the joint-stock company
items.i6: Strengthening regulation of transactions with affiliated persons and major transactions
items.i7: Protection of shareholders from dilution of their stakes and rights

items.i11: Issues of information disclosure and transparency in the field of sustainable development
items.i12: Ensuring law enforcement that protects the interests of minority shareholders

items.i0: Development and adoption of the Law of the Republic of Uzbekistan «On Artificial Intelligence»

items.i3: Development of local management companies and investment funds

items.i0: Establishment of an Interdepartmental Working Group on circular economy issues and an annual specialized ESG award within the Council
items.i1: Introduction of the national decision-making quality standard (Tayanch Uzbekistan)
items.i2: Introduction of the national regulatory impact assessment system (RIA) and mandatory ex-post review
items.i3: Introduction of the national Pre-Mortem and After-Action Review (AAR) system for mega-projects (RBC Strategic Review System)
items.i4: Formation of the national KPI, accountability and «attention architecture» system in the context of RBC (Uzbekistan–2030)
items.i5: Introduction of the national supplier qualification standard based on RBC principles
items.i6: Development of the national RBC policy and complaints review mechanism (based on the NCP model)
items.i7: Creation of a national system for reviewing complaints and resolving disputes on RBC principles (RBC Grievance Mechanism)
items.i8: Introduction of uniform ESG information disclosure standards for companies
items.i9: Establishment of criteria for including organizations in the Register of Responsible Business Conduct
items.i10: Introduction of requirements for climate planning and climate risk management at the company level
items.i11: Creation of the legal and institutional framework for issuing «green sukuk»
items.i12: Development of national labeling standards and methodology for «green» loans
items.i13: Development of prudential incentive measures for green and sustainable lending
items.i14: Development of a comprehensive register of green and sustainable projects («Green Projects Book»)
items.i15: Development of the National Sustainable Finance Strategy of the Republic of Uzbekistan
items.i16: Development of ESG ratings of state enterprises and public issuers at global agencies
items.i17: Modernization of environmental and social standards in the energy sector taking into account international experience
items.i18: Granting the investor ownership rights to environmental attributes

initiatives.heading: Initiatives to improve the investment climate

intro.p1: The Secretariat of the Foreign Investors Council is a permanently functioning operational body that ensures the continuity and consistency of the Council's work between sessions. The Secretariat organizes plenary and interim meetings, coordinates the activities of the sixteen working groups, maintains a daily dialogue with Council members and foreign investors, collects and analyzes initiatives to improve the investment climate, and ensures their advancement into the relevant state policy.
intro.p2: With the acquisition of legal entity status, the Secretariat gained the institutional autonomy necessary to effectively fulfil its mandate and to engage with international financial institutions.

aziz.description: Doctor of Economic Sciences with over 20 years of experience in corporate governance, investment policy, and strategic consulting. He has extensive experience working with international investors, development banks, and government institutions. He leads the development of investor relations, policy coordination, and institutional development.

He Leads The Development Of Investor Relations, Policy Coordination, And Institutional Development.

vladislav.description: Program Coordinator of the Secretariat of the Foreign Investors Council under the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan. Vladislav Li is a specialist in process coordination, stakeholder engagement, and ensuring effective communication. He possesses practical skills in client relations, event organization, and project support.
vladislav.duties:
Program Coordinator
Secretariat Of The Foreign Investors Council Under The President Of The Republic Of Uzbekistan

About The Position
The Program Coordinator Is Responsible For Organizing, Coordinating, And Monitoring The Projects And Initiatives Of The Foreign Investors Council, Ensuring Effective Cooperation Between Government Agencies, International Partners, And The Business Community. This Role Involves Overseeing Program Implementation, Supporting Operational Processes, And Ensuring The Timely Execution Of Tasks Within The Scope Of The Secretariat's Activities.

Key Responsibilities:

Project And Program Coordination
• Planning And Monitoring FIC Projects At All Stages Of Implementation.
• Monitoring The Execution Of Tasks, Deadlines, And Key Milestones (Control Points).
• Facilitating Interaction Between Internal And External Project Participants.
• Supporting The Preparation And Management Of Events, Meetings, And Working Groups.

Operational Activities
• Organizing The Daily Workflows Of The Secretariat.
• Preparing Meeting Materials, Agendas, And Minutes.
• Ensuring Effective Document Flow And Monitoring The Implementation Of Assignments.
• Coordinating Interaction Between Departments And Stakeholders.

Communication And Collaboration
• Maintaining Constant Communication With Foreign Investors And Partners.
• Ensuring The Prompt Exchange Of Information Between All Parties.
• Preparing Official Correspondence And Working Materials In Multiple Languages.
• Assisting In The Development Of Sustainable Professional Relationships.

Analytics And Reporting
• Collecting And Systematizing Information Regarding Projects And Initiatives.
• Preparing Regular Progress Reports On The Status Of Task Execution.
• Monitoring Progress And Identifying Potential Risks.

Event Organization
• Coordinating FIC Business Events, Forums, And High-Level Meetings.
• Supporting Logistics And Administrative Processes.
• Overseeing The Preparation Of Participants And Relevant Materials.

Core Principles
• High Professionalism And Precision In Task Execution.
• Maintaining An Official And Diplomatic Tone In All Communications.
• Strict Adherence To Confidentiality And Ethical Standards.

jurabek.duties:
Strategic Communications Expert
Secretariat Of The Foreign Investors Council Under The President Of The Republic Of Uzbekistan

About The Position
The Advisor Is Responsible For Strengthening The International Image And Public Visibility Of The Foreign Investors Council (FIC), Developing Its Digital Presence, And Managing Strategic Communications With Foreign Investors, International Financial Institutions, And A Broad Audience In Uzbek, Russian, And English.

Key Responsibilities:

Strategy And Analytics
•  Conducting A Digital Presence Audit Of The FIC Secretariat.
•  Developing And Implementing An International SMM Strategy Aligned With The FIC's Objectives And Target Audience.
•  Selecting And Justifying The Optimal Platforms (LinkedIn, Telegram, Instagram, Facebook).

Content Development And Publication
•  Preparing Monthly Multilingual Content: Analytical Posts, Expert Commentary, Infographics, Visual Storytelling, And Event Announcements.
•  Producing Short Videos (Interviews, Event Coverage, Initiative Digests) And Animated Clips Regarding Reforms And Key Updates.
•  Establishing And Maintaining A Unified Visual Identity Across The FIC's Digital Platforms.

Promotion And Account Management
•  Daily Management Of The FIC's Social Media: Planning, Posting, Moderating Discussions, And Responding To Comments In A Diplomatic Tone.
•  Launching And Optimizing Paid Advertising Campaigns.
•  Weekly Monitoring Of Performance And Tactical Adjustment Of Communications.

Reporting And Analytics
•  Monthly Analytical Reports On Reach, Engagement, And Post Effectiveness.
•  Developing Strategic Recommendations For The Growth Of Digital Communication Channels.
•  Technical Support For Key Online Events And Live Broadcasts.

Core Principles
•  Compliance With International Standards Of Strategic Digital Communication.
•  Official And Diplomatic Tone Of Communication.
•  Strict Adherence To Confidentiality And Ethical Standards.

madina.duties:
Administrative Officer Of The Secretariat Of The Foreign Investors Council Under The President Of The Republic Of Uzbekistan

Professional Experience And Key Responsibilities:

Document Flow And Clerical Work
• Managing Incoming And Outgoing Correspondence Of The Secretariat In Uzbek, Russian, And English.
• Registration, Systematization, And Archiving Of Documents In Accordance With Established Regulations.
• Preparation Of Official Letters, Certificates, Minutes, And Internal Memos.
• Monitoring The Timely Execution Of Assignments And Compliance With Document Processing Deadlines.
• Ensuring The Confidentiality And Security Of Official Information.

Organization Of Events And Meetings
• Planning And Organizational Support For Working Group Meetings, Plenary Sessions, And Bilateral Meetings.
• Managing Event Logistics: Hall Bookings, Preparation Of Materials, And Technical Support.
• Drafting Agendas, Taking Minutes Of Meetings, And Monitoring The Implementation Of Decisions.
• Liaising With Foreign Delegations And Ensuring Protocol Support For Official Visits.

Coordination And Analysis
• Ensuring Prompt Communication Between Investors And Ministries, Receiving And Monitoring The Processing Of Investor Inquiries.
• Preparing Regular Administrative Reports On Secretariat Activities And Presentation Materials For Management.
• Technical Support For Online Events And Video Conferences.

Competencies And Languages:
• Languages: Uzbek And Russian — Fluent; English — Professional.
• Professional Skills: State Clerical Standards, Strategic And Diplomatic Communication, Professional Proficiency In MS Office Programs.
• Personal Qualities: Multitasking, Stress Tolerance, Result-Oriented, And Client-Focused Approach.

members.intro: The members of the Foreign Investors Council are leading international companies, banks and financial institutions making strategic investments in the economy of Uzbekistan. Membership reflects a long-term commitment to the country’s development and provides a direct channel of dialogue with the Government on investment policy matters. The Council brings together 41 members from 19 countries, representing 23 sectors of the economy.

join.title: Become a Member
join.subtitle: Categories, Influence and Admission Procedure
join.lead: The FIC is the principal institutional platform for structured dialogue between foreign investors and the Government of Uzbekistan.

admission.heading: Admission Procedure
admission.note: Admission and participation formats are determined in accordance with the Charter, the Membership Regulations and applicable protocol rules.

application.title: Application and Documents
application.text: Submission of a written application together with a complete set of required supporting documents, specifying the requested membership category

review.title: Review
review.text: Assessment, including verification of documents and compliance with requirements

decision.title: Decision
decision.text: Consideration and decision by the Executive Committee

formalization.title: Formalization
formalization.text: Signing of the membership agreement and payment of membership fees

tiers.heading: Membership Categories and Level of Influence

exec.title: Executive Committee / Founders (IFIs)
points: Decisive vote on amendments to the Charter, reorganization and liquidation

full.title: Full Members
points: Participation in plenary sessions with the President of Uzbekistan
Participation in the official protocol and closed working formats

observer.title: Observers
points: Participation in open sessions, discussions and expert formats

participation.heading: Principles of Participation
items: FIC plenary sessions are open to members and observers
Official protocol formats are conducted within the framework of membership
Decision-making and voting are reserved for members only

eligibility.heading: Key Eligibility Criteria
general: Alignment with FIC objectives and a positive business reputation
Active or planned investment activity in Uzbekistan
fullMembers: Investments or projects amounting to ≥ 50 million USD
For IFIs: financing / guarantees / technical assistance ≥ 500 million USD
